subject: Science
chapter: Nature of Matter: Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

key_points: Matter can be classified into mixtures and pure substances
Mixtures contain two or more substances that retain their individual properties
Pure substances include elements and compounds
Elements consist of only one type of atom and cannot be broken down chemically
Compounds are formed when elements combine chemically in fixed proportions
examples: Air is a mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and other gases
Salt dissolved in water forms a uniform mixture called a solution
Water (H2O) is a compound made of hydrogen and oxygen
Carbon and oxygen combine to form carbon dioxide
Steel is an alloy made by mixing iron with carbon
formulas_or_processes: Formation of compound: Iron + Sulfur → Iron sulfide
Mixture separation using filtration: Solid + liquid → separated solid residue and filtrate
Evaporation: Solution → solvent evaporates leaving solute behind
exam_tips: Understand the difference between homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures
Remember that compounds have fixed composition while mixtures do not
Revise examples of elements, compounds, and mixtures
Be able to explain simple separation techniques such as filtration and evaporation
